steps.title: Set up the calculation
steps.content: At \$2 per pound, the cost for $c$ pounds is found by multiplying the rate by the quantity.

steps.title: Multiply to find the total cost
steps.content:
$$2 \times c = 2c \text{ dollars}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. If one pound of grapes costs \$2, two pounds of grapes will cost 2 times \$2, three pounds of grapes will cost 3 times \$2, and so on. Therefore, c pounds of grapes will cost c times \$2, which is 2c dollars.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from incorrectly adding instead of multiplying. Choice C is incorrect and may result from assuming that c pounds cost \$2, and then finding the cost per pound. Choice D is incorrect and could result from incorrectly assuming that 2 pounds cost \$c, and then finding the cost per pound.
